modules.label: init · scaffolder
modules.seam: the sole entry the whole product boots from; varies by install layout (wheel core/_seeds vs dev .meeseeks-config)
modules.iface: init_workspace(root, force) → InitResult; idempotent (skip-not-overwrite), two content sources (hardcoded templates floor + bundle-copy ceiling), registers workspace in app.db

modules.label: init · templates
modules.seam: data-only; the write list in seed.py must name every key here
modules.iface: pure YAML/MD/Python string blobs + template_files() write list; channel rosters live here, not the bundle

modules.label: init · paths
modules.seam: path resolution; .meeseeks-config suffix special-case
modules.iface: resolve_workspace_root() + workspace_layout(); thin wrapper over core.config constants

modules.label: dispatcher
modules.seam: the graph topology; agent nodes are closures over run_agent_turn
modules.iface: Dispatcher.stream(user_text, thread_id, channel_id) over a LangGraph StateGraph; every agent node feeds back to the router; invariant: all turns route through route_decide. Now also accepts a per-channel session_store (typed Any) and injects it into configurable['session_store'] so deep-provider adapters read it off the InvocationContext seam instead of importing core.orchestrator.channels.
modules.tests: test_dispatcher.py (init only). 0.96 LINE coverage is incidental via integration; graph topology/routing feedback never asserted at the Dispatcher.stream interface in a unit test.

modules.label: run_agent_turn
modules.seam: run_agent_turn IS the single owner of a turn (the dispatcher just calls it and returns its Command); helpers (parse_mentions/build_next_plan/tag_with_speaker) are already separate testable seams. Already deep (0.9) — an AgentTurn class would be a cosmetic rename failing the deletion test.
modules.iface: async run_agent_turn(state, config, name, spec, peers, role, middleware): scope transcript → invoke agent → parse @mentions → send_message tool → stream events → next RoutingPlan
modules.tests: test_dispatcher_agent_turn.py (13 tests via a FakeAgent crossing the run_agent_turn seam: transcript scoping, @mention->RoutingPlan, fan-out self-mention isolation, error path) + integration. DONE.

modules.label: router · route_decide
modules.seam: sole router implementation; team-middleware hints branch here
modules.iface: route_decide(state, agents, peer_graph) → Command; cascade: pending route → user-just-spoke → bounce-back stack → hop cap → missing agent → END; resolve_peer_names()

modules.label: routing pkg
modules.seam: pure functions — fully testable; the one deep-tested corner of the orchestrator
modules.iface: parse_first/all_mentions, find_dropped_mentions, find_prose_delegation (quoted/case/space-insensitive); RoutingPlan dataclass + last-writer-wins reducer; select_next_agent

modules.label: team middleware
modules.seam: hierarchy rules concentrated here; invoked by router_ops + agent_turn
modules.iface: TeamMiddleware wraps PURE module-level functions (VERIFIED: push/pop_caller, should_bounce_back, is_escape_upward, next_callers_after_turn -- explicit args over RoutingPlan, no I/O, no hidden state). Encodes caller stacks, push-self, bounce-back, escape-upward vs hallucination.
modules.tests: test_team_middleware.py (20 unit tests at the pure-function interface; mutation-verified). DONE.

modules.label: agent_node_helpers
modules.seam: transcript-scoping for parallel fan-out; under-tested at unit level
modules.iface: channel_to_context (scope transcript to one agent, last 50), parse_mentions, tag_with_speaker, _scope_to_self_mention (isolate per-worker task in fan-out)

modules.label: send_message tool
modules.seam: directed-message routing + budget; self-contained
modules.iface: make_send_message_tool(sender, recipients) → @tool that appends tagged AIMessage + routes Command; per-turn outbound budget (default 8)

modules.label: channel_manager
modules.seam: the assembly point for a multi-agent dispatcher; builder/cache/checkpoint as mixins, planner+peer_graph as helpers
modules.iface: ChannelManager.get_dispatcher(channel_id) builds+caches a Dispatcher; build pipeline: channel yaml -> resolve teams -> load agents -> compute peer_graph -> AgentSpecs; hot-swap checkpointer invalidates cache. The builder resolves the per-channel CLI session store (get_session_store(channel_id, config_dir)) and hands it to the Dispatcher, which forwards it to provider adapters through the invocation seam (closes the old provider->orch-channels leak).

modules.label: channels persistence
modules.seam: the disk/YAML boundary for channels; also owns the session-id store that providers reach into
modules.iface: load/save/delete/list_channels + resolve_roster (flatten roster+teams → AgentConfigs); ChannelConfig/RosterEntry pydantic; ChannelSessionStore (per-channel CLI session ids in sessions.json)

modules.label: teams
modules.seam: team-yaml I/O; consumed by channel_manager planner
modules.iface: TeamConfig (leader + workers) + load/save/resolve_teams; member_slugs/team_ids derived post-load
modules.tests: indirect (channel-manager tests). 0.90 LINE coverage is incidental; teams.py I/O is never asserted through its own load/save/resolve interface.

modules.label: threads + thread_db
modules.seam: the LangGraph checkpoint DB; distinct from the app DB in core/api/repositories
modules.iface: ThreadManager.open(db_path) async ctx → .checkpointer + thread metadata; thread_db owns SQLite schema, WAL, serialized writer (avoids 'database is locked'); encode_thread_id namespaces by channel

modules.label: api · app/lifespan
modules.seam: process lifecycle; register_routers wires the 16 router packages
modules.iface: FastAPI lifespan owns ThreadManager+ChannelManager singletons, loads secrets once, boots service supervisor + scheduler; ordering is load-bearing

modules.label: api · state singletons
modules.seam: the import anchor that breaks the app→routers→services→app cycle; routers/services MUST import managers here, not from app
modules.iface: get_channel_manager/get_thread_manager process singletons; get_thread_manager re-resolves active workspace's threads DB each call + repoints on hot-swap (double-checked locking)
modules.tests: test_api_state_hotswap.py (8 tests: swap branch via patched resolve_threads_db, double-checked lock, CM repoint for moved + same workspace). DONE.

modules.label: api · repositories
modules.seam: the only place that should hold SQL for the app DB — but several routers bypass it
modules.iface: app DB core: _connection owns get_conn/new_id/init_schema/migrations; runs_repo + workspace_repo + settings_repo are thin CRUD; sqlite.py facade. The actions / build_runs / build_specs tables now have their OWN repo nodes (api-repo-actions / api-repo-build-runs / api-repo-build-specs).
modules.tests: test_connection.py, test_runs_repo.py, test_workspace_repo.py, test_settings_repo.py. PR4: the last two router layer-skips (runs delete + activity) now go through run_service -- nothing bypasses the seam.

modules.label: api · schemas
modules.seam: the HTTP wire contract; snapshot-tested in tests/contract
modules.iface: pydantic request/response models per domain (chat, agents, runs, ...); pure contract, no imports from routers/services/repos

modules.label: router · chat (WS)
modules.seam: the live chat WS turn loop; delegates seq allocation to the seq-allocator seam
modules.iface: WebSocket turn loop: canonical thread-id validation, dual drain (langgraph stream + event-bus) racing on ONE shared _SeqAllocator, speaker extraction, per-turn auto-title. VERIFIED: seq allocation stays sync on the event loop (only the DB write is offloaded) so the two drains can't double-allocate.

modules.label: router · runs
modules.seam: mission submission/streaming; delete is a layer-skip past run_service (NOT raw SQL)
modules.iface: run CRUD + WS stream + cancel; _helpers owns the mission worker coroutine, event sink, single in-flight slot via its OWN asyncio.Lock (PR6). delete_run now routes through run_service (PR4); the in-flight _active guard stays in the router.

modules.label: router · build_runs
modules.seam: thin HTTP/WS surface over BuildRunService; no longer reaches into build_runs_repo and holds no slot/worker state
modules.iface: build-run CRUD + WS stream; every endpoint delegates to get_build_run_service(). _helpers shrank to two pure wire helpers (_event_to_payload, _wait_for_client_close); typed errors map to HTTP via the global handler.

modules.label: router · agents
modules.seam: agent admin surface; _helpers monkey-patchable via package re-export
modules.iface: agent CRUD: create/save delegate to agent_write_service (typed errors, 403-protected system agents); read/health via agent_service; _stats aggregation (percentiles, token buckets)

modules.label: router · actions
modules.seam: scheduled-mission admin; thin parse→delegate→format over action_service
modules.iface: action CRUD + cron schedule-preview; delegates all logic to action_service → actions_repo; record→ActionRow via _to_action_row. No SQL in the router

modules.label: router · workspaces
modules.seam: the workspace filesystem surface; subprocess + symlink edges untested
modules.iface: workspace CRUD + filesystem tree + file read/write/run (subprocess w/ timeout) + init; path-traversal validation + secret-scan on edits

modules.label: router · threads
modules.seam: thread admin; clean delegation to thread_service
modules.iface: thread CRUD + per-channel thread router; canonical thread-id encode/decode; orphan-filtering invariant lives in thread_service

modules.label: router · settings
modules.seam: config surface; validators mirror provider rules
modules.iface: settings KV + secret mgmt + per-setting validators + schema endpoint; defaults.py curates the settings catalog

modules.label: router · channels
modules.seam: channel admin; thin over orchestrator.channels
modules.iface: channel CRUD + health rollup (per-agent CLI build status); invalidates ChannelManager cache on edit

modules.label: router · marketplace
modules.seam: marketplace read surface; filesystem walk in _helpers
modules.iface: read-only aggregation of installed MCPs (across agent yamls + shared files, deduped) + skills (five known SKILL.md locations)

modules.label: router · services
modules.seam: services admin; delegates to supervisor
modules.iface: supervised-process CRUD + restart + logs; thin wrapper over ServiceSupervisor

modules.label: routers · metadata
modules.seam: assorted read endpoints; activity bypasses run_service
modules.iface: read-only metadata: backends, providers, teams CRUD, health, activity feed. activity now routes through run_service.recent_activity (PR4) -- no longer reaches runs_repo directly.

modules.label: svc · build_engine
modules.seam: the deterministic build state machine; the heart of the Stateful Builder redesign
modules.iface: BuildEngine(spec, sink, cancel).run() drives confirm→provision→scaffold→verify→finalize; LLM-driven steps borrow the process-global MISSION_LOCK; emits builder_ui events

modules.label: svc · build_provisioners
modules.seam: the build-specific adapter over the agent + team services; seam now points DOWN
modules.iface: create/update_agent_item + create/update_team_item for BuildEngine; calls agent_write_service / team_service (no router import); owns spec→config mapping + PATCH-not-replace + (state,note) normalization via to_http_status()

modules.label: svc · build_spec
modules.seam: spec validation seam; rules duplicated against the frontend
modules.iface: build-spec CRUD + dry-run validate (name/slug uniqueness, agent existence, team lead+workers, secret scan, mirrors frontend rules); build_spec_chat does LLM-driven goal/spec generation

suggestion.title: Split BuildSpecService's five concerns (defer — premature)
suggestion.problem: Verified premature: build_spec_service.py is 292 lines (<350 ceiling), the five concerns (CRUD / validate / draft / importable / chat) share only the _workspace_id helper, and the chat concern is ALREADY extracted to build_spec_chat.py. Active build-engine WIP is editing both files now — splitting today risks merge conflicts.
suggestion.solution: Defer. Revisit once the WIP lands AND the file nears ~320 lines or grows a 6th concern; then extract a BuildSpecValidator (validate-before-write is the natural seam) and keep CRUD on the service. No independent seams today.
wins: validate-before-write is the real seam when split

modules.label: svc · agent (read)
modules.seam: agent read/health seam; mutations live in the router not here
modules.iface: read-side: agent_dir_for_name, prompt_meta (file/inline/default resolution chain), enabled_mcp_servers, compute_health (secrets + MCP files + CLI binary, ordered checks). No mutations

modules.label: svc · thread
modules.seam: thread ops seam; the orphan-filter invariant is untested
modules.iface: async facade over ThreadManager; list/new/rename/delete/export; invariant: legacy raw thr_* ids filtered from lists (can never grow checkpoints)
modules.tests: test_thread_service.py. 0.85 LINE coverage, but the legacy thr_* orphan-filter invariant is not asserted at the interface.

modules.label: svc · run
modules.seam: shallow wrapper — deletion test: complexity just moves to runs_repo
modules.iface: error-aware CRUD facade over runs_repo + RunNotFoundError; now also owns delete_run + recent_activity (PR4) so routers/scheduler never touch the runs tables directly.
modules.tests: test_run_service.py (incl. delete_run + recent_activity delegation) + via runs router.

modules.label: svc · workspace
modules.seam: shallow wrapper over workspace_repo
modules.iface: typed CRUD facade over workspace_repo + WorkspaceNotFound/Conflict/PathError; thin

modules.label: api · scheduler
modules.seam: the cron poll loop; SQL lives behind the repo/service seam
modules.iface: polls actions_repo.list_due(now); runs_repo.has_active_run guards in-flight; action_service advances schedule + renders goal; dispatches via runs.start_run_internal (deferred). No raw SQL

modules.label: agents · factory
modules.seam: orchestration glue — deletion test: its two steps migrate cleanly to callers
modules.iface: build_agent(config, mode, runtime_context) → BuiltAgent; resolves system prompt ONCE at build time with live context, checks secrets, lazy-builds deep provider

modules.label: agents · validate
modules.seam: the role-rules seam; replaced 3 legacy call sites (composer, router, init)
modules.iface: validate_agent_config(config, invocation_mode): single home for role taxonomy (ROLE_ORDER), conflict matrix, invocation-mode role synthesis; derive_agent_roles() for the channel planner; raises structured AgentValidationError

modules.label: config · loader
modules.seam: the agent-yaml -> AgentConfig boundary; runtime callers now go through AgentConfigLoader(workspace)
modules.iface: AgentConfigLoader(workspace).load(slug)/.load_path(dir) owns shared-defaults + shared_dir resolution (was repeated at 4 call sites); load_agent_config stays the low-level primitive for discover_agents + init + schema-only tests

modules.label: core · types
modules.seam: the schema floor everything imports; pure data
modules.iface: central pydantic registry: AgentConfig, LLMConfig, RuntimeConfig, MCPConfig (transport validation), WorkspaceConfig; provider:model string format

modules.label: prompts · composer
modules.seam: prompt assembly seam; resolved ONCE in the factory (PR5)
modules.iface: resolve_system_prompt(config, runtime_context) is the single resolution point; build_system_prompt now REQUIRES base_prompt (no context-dropping fallback). The shared-dir resolver is INJECTED (set_shared_dir_resolver) -- core/prompts no longer imports core.api (leak closed, PR5).
modules.tests: test_prompt_composer.py, test_prompt_resolution.py; check_deps green (no core->api edge). DONE.

modules.label: core · secrets
modules.seam: the secret-presence gate before an agent starts
modules.iface: ensure_secrets(agent, required) raises MissingSecretsError; loads .env once; has_secret/collect_missing_required; secrets_scan flags hardcoded keys in yaml/files

modules.label: providers · base
modules.seam: the provider-selection seam; invocation now flows through the typed InvocationContext, not an untyped config['configurable'] dict
modules.iface: DeepProviderRunner Protocol + build_deep_provider factory + InvocationContext/SessionRef typed seam (now carries a SessionStore port the orchestrator injects, so adapters never reach up for session persistence) + verdict/text/prompt helpers

modules.label: providers · claude_code
modules.seam: the claude-agent-sdk adapter; the CLI-session store is injected via ctx.session.store — no longer imports core.orchestrator.channels / core.api.workspace_resolver
modules.iface: ClaudeCodeRunner.ainvoke: per-thread subprocess client pool keyed by session-id+slug, session resume, stream dedup (whitespace/chunk-repeat), MCPConfig→SDK options, thinking budget, anti-loop tool guard, cancellation

modules.label: providers · codex
modules.seam: the codex-CLI adapter; isolated from claude_code (no cross-import); CLI-session store injected via ctx.session.store — no longer imports core.orchestrator / core.api
modules.iface: CodexRunner.ainvoke: shells out to codex CLI; build_argv serializes config to TOML (workspace/model/reasoning/MCP), session resume, JSONL event parse, error extraction

modules.label: providers · dashboard_mcp
modules.seam: the bridge from a CLI subprocess back into in-process builder UI; untested
modules.iface: in-process MCP servers (builder_ui, draft_ui) callable from CLI subprocesses. VERIFIED: each handler is a pure async def(args: dict)->dict funneling through _publish, which reads the BUILDER_UI_THREAD_ID contextvar and calls event_bus.publish(tid, kind, {sub,...}); server.py only wraps them with the SDK @tool decorator. Testable with no subprocess.
modules.tests: test_deep_provider_dashboard_mcp.py (14 tests driving each handler -> event_bus directly, no subprocess) + test_dashboard_mcp_handlers.py (mocked). DONE.

modules.label: providers · event_bus
modules.seam: the streaming hub every live surface routes through
modules.iface: per-thread pub/sub bus; dispatcher/providers/builder_ui publish streaming + status events, the chat WS drains them; kept separate to avoid dispatcher↔provider cycles

modules.label: mcp · loader
modules.seam: the MCP-server assembly seam; lazy-imports services.loader
modules.iface: collect_enabled_mcp_servers(mcps, agent_dir): agent.yaml servers + service auto-injection (sse) + shared files; env resolution; stdio path resolution; wraps tools via safety

modules.label: mcp · safety
modules.seam: tool-call safety wrapper; self-contained
modules.iface: _wrap_tool_for_safety: any tool exception → readable string; anti-loop guard (same tool+args >5× → stop); handles content_and_artifact tuples

modules.label: cli · runner + cmds
modules.seam: the Typer command surface; runner.py is wiring only, bodies in sibling modules
modules.iface: Typer app (runner.py) + subcommand bodies: chat-once (menu), channel chat/create/list. Heavy imports deferred to keep --help fast. Delegates the interactive loop to cli-repl and terminal input to cli-input.

modules.label: cli · event stream
modules.seam: DEFERRED on inspection: format.py (in-process decode events) and streaming/events.py (mission-lifecycle JSONL, persisted+polled) are different transports with different kind-sets, not the same vocab twice. A true unify changes the WS contract or the stored-run JSONL format (back-compat) + threads agent_path through the mission on_event chain — large/cross-cutting, do as a dedicated change.
modules.iface: AgentEventStream(agent).run(messages) decodes LangGraph chunks → typed events (Token/ToolStart/ToolEnd/TurnEnd/Subagent/Thinking); but requires an agent ref even when only decoding

suggestion.title: Unify the CLI and API event vocabularies
suggestion.problem: Verified two vocabularies: core/cli/events/format.py dataclasses (Token/Thinking/ToolStart/ToolEnd/Subagent*/TurnEnd/TurnError — all carry agent_path) vs core/streaming/events.py StreamEmitter JSONL (same kinds, NO agent_path), so mission JSONL drops subagent lanes. The chat WS payloads already carry agent_path, so the gap is only on the mission/JSONL path.
suggestion.solution: One TurnEvent vocabulary + an EventCodec (serialize/deserialize) feeding StreamEmitter, the chat WS, and the CLI renderer. Version the JSONL so stored runs still parse. Deletion test: moderate yes. Effort M, medium risk (JSONL versioning + back-compat for old runs).
wins: agent_path lands in mission JSONL too
round-trip codec is testable; test surface shrinks

modules.label: cli · renderer
modules.seam: the live terminal display state machine; consumes cli-events
modules.iface: ChatRenderer live per-turn display (tool rows, footer, refresh throttle); must dedupe tool starts (_announced_tool_ids) because the stream yields growing tool_calls lists

modules.label: services · supervisor
modules.seam: the process-lifecycle owner for declared services; _pkg() indirection for test patching
modules.iface: ServiceSupervisor singleton: start_all spawns autostart services, watches service.yaml mtimes, probes health (HTTP/TCP/cmd), restarts on crash, SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL shutdown

modules.label: services · loader
modules.seam: service-spec discovery + schema; the two identical attachment walks (team/channel) now share _load_attachments(subdir, id, config_dir). services_for_agent / routable kept as distinct typed entry points (the broad one-resolver was rejected as over-reach).
modules.iface: discover specs at services/<name>/service.yaml → ServiceConfig; services_for_agent resolves attachments; registry + proxy URL calc; ServiceConfig pydantic is the load-bearing contract

modules.label: mission · runner
modules.seam: the goal-driven framing + budget loop; reads the verdict from mission-core's global slot
modules.iface: run_mission(built, goal, on_event) → MissionResult; loops agent.astream until complete_mission tool fires or a budget trips (max_iterations/seconds/tool_calls); collects workspace files

modules.label: mission · core
modules.seam: per-mission verdict capture via a contextvar-held mutable box (proven correct + tested). DEFERRED VerdictScope: the full version (delete free fns, scope-only surface) would churn 5 test files incl. the concurrency proof itself, for zero functional gain; the compat-preserving version is cosmetic. Do only as a dedicated polish PR.
modules.iface: complete_mission / CLI record_verdict MUTATE the _VerdictBox bound to the current context (never rebind). run_mission sets a fresh box per run (token + reset in finally); a _GLOBAL_BOX default keeps direct callers working. MISSION_LOCK is GONE; the runs router owns its own single-in-flight lock.
modules.tests: test_mission.py (global-box compat) + test_mission_verdict_scope.py (concurrent isolation, nested no-leak; mutation-verified non-vacuous). DONE.

suggestion.title: Encapsulate the verdict box in a VerdictScope (defer — polish)
suggestion.problem: Verified: correctness is done + tested (concurrent isolation, nested no-leak). The 'mutate-in-place, never reassign' rule is only a comment, spread across mission.core + mission.runner + deep_providers.base. A wrapper that leaves the free functions in place is cosmetic — it only enforces the invariant by construction if the free functions are deleted and the scope is injected (L effort across 3 modules). No bug is removed.
suggestion.solution: Defer unless already editing mission.*. If done, do it fully: delete _capture_verdict/take_pending_verdict/reset_verdict, make VerdictScope the only surface, thread it via InvocationContext. Pure locality refinement on already-correct, well-tested behaviour.
wins: invariant enforced by construction (only if free funcs deleted)
no correctness gain — polish only; risk is regressing working isolation

modules.label: builder_ui
modules.seam: the live-card protocol; emits to event_bus, set up by the dispatcher
modules.iface: draft protocol (draft_open/status/field/commit → live cards) + builder tools (submit/undo/redo/search_mcps); thread routed via BUILDER_UI_THREAD_ID contextvar; CardKind Literal must match frontend card registry
modules.tests: integration only. 0.79 LINE coverage via dispatcher/provider runs; the draft-card protocol is never driven through its own tool interface in a unit test.

modules.label: streaming · events
modules.seam: liveness-signal emission; defers a schema import to dodge a cycle
modules.iface: emit_turn_status(bus, phase, ...) publishes typed turn-status events (routing/thinking/streaming/tool_running/terminated/heartbeat); phases validated against a frozenset that also lives in the schema
modules.tests: no dedicated test. 0.82 LINE coverage is incidental via chat-protocol integration; emit_turn_status phases never asserted at the interface.

modules.label: skills
modules.seam: skill discovery; pure path enumeration
modules.iface: discover_skill_files (scan dirs, skip plain README) + resolve_skill_paths (cross-backend catalog: ~/.claude/skills, ~/.codex/skills, workspace shared); hardcoded source list, no plugin point

modules.label: pricing
modules.seam: cost math; pure lookups + arithmetic
modules.iface: lookup_price(provider, model) + compute_cost(tokens...) from a YAML price index ((provider,*) wildcard); returns None for unknown/CLI providers; _normalise_model strips provider prefix

modules.label: actions_repo
modules.seam: the single owner of actions-table SQL — the seam the router + scheduler now go through
modules.iface: pure SQL for the actions table: insert/get/list/list_upcoming/list_due(now)/update(allowlisted cols)/delete/advance_schedule → ActionRecord; imports _connection only

modules.label: svc · action
modules.seam: the actions domain-logic seam; router parses, scheduler polls, both delegate here
modules.iface: ActionService: schedule validation + next-run recompute + create/update field-diff + goal rendering + advance-for-dispatch; typed ActionNotFoundError (404) like run_service

modules.label: svc · agent_write
modules.seam: the agent-mutation seam that points DOWN — router + build pipeline both call it
modules.iface: create_agent/save_agent + validators (reject_secrets, validate_agent_yaml_text, slug_exists); raises typed MeeseeksError (409/403/422/400) the global handler maps; returns the agent name, not AgentDetail

modules.label: svc · team
modules.seam: the team-mutation seam; teams router + build pipeline both call it
modules.iface: create_team/update_team/delete_team + validate payload + channel-invalidation + TeamCreate/UpdateRequest + typed 409 errors; moved out of the teams router

modules.label: fe · api types
modules.seam: the wire contract; the single seam a backend schema change ripples through
modules.iface: openapi.generated.ts (codegen from FastAPI app.openapi()) + types.ts (ergonomic re-exports). CRITICAL: the WebSocket envelope unions (ServerEvent / ClientEvent / BuilderUIEvent / DraftUIEvent / CardKind) are HAND-WRITTEN in types.ts, NOT generated — they mirror backend emitters with no codegen safety net.
modules.tests: test_ws_contract_drift.py (3 pytest: backend get_args(CardKind) / ServerEvent kinds / draft subs == frontend types.ts unions, aligned 10/19/6) + chatStore & draftUiStore assertNever exhaustiveness (a new backend kind is now a TS compile error + a failing test). DONE.

modules.label: fe · api client
modules.seam: the HTTP/WS request seam; the only place fetch lives
modules.iface: apiClient {get/post/put/patch/delete} (throws ApiError on non-2xx) + wsUrl() builds the chat/build-run WS URLs. payloads.ts holds the ServerEvent type-guards (isAiText/isTurnStatus/...) + artifact extraction.

modules.label: fe · query hooks
modules.seam: the Tanstack-Query data layer; one file per backend domain
modules.iface: per-domain useQuery/useMutation hooks (agents/channels/teams/buildSpecs/buildRuns/marketplace/workspaces/settings/...); query-key registry + invalidate-on-success. Components never call apiClient directly.
modules.tests: ~1.7% line coverage — effectively UNTESTED; exercised only incidentally.

modules.label: fe · chat WS client
modules.seam: the live chat socket lifecycle; frontend counterpart of api-router-chat
modules.iface: ChatClient.connect/send/cancel/ping for one (channel,thread); stamps every send with last_seq watermark for replay; treats close 1008 + fatal frames as terminal (no retry); exponential backoff (max 30s) otherwise. Fires onEvent/onFatal/onStatusChange.

modules.label: fe · chat reducer
modules.seam: the pure ServerEvent→ChatState reducer; the deep, well-tested core of the chat UI
modules.iface: reduceChat(state, ServerEvent)→ChatState: dedup by seq, route assistant events into bubbles / parallel groups, extract artifacts + activity, hydrate from ThreadHistory. useTurnStatus tracks phase + 15s stale flag. Mirrors the backend ServerEvent protocol (hand-kept in sync).

modules.label: fe · channel chat UI
modules.seam: the channel chat surface; owns the ChatClient + reducer lifecycle
modules.iface: ChannelChat instantiates ChatClient + useReducer per thread, feeds events to reducer + useTurnStatus, renders ChannelMessages (bubbles/parallel/artifact cards) + ChannelWorkbench (topology + activity) + composer with @mention autocomplete.
modules.tests: presentational; mostly 0% — logic lives in the reducer

modules.label: fe · build-spec model
modules.seam: the pure BuildSpec model + form; the spec-validation seam mirrored against the backend
modules.iface: SpecItem union (Agent|Mcp|Team) + constructors + validateSpec() + YAML (de)serialize, no React/fetch. PlanEditor/AskPanel bind the form. INVARIANT: SpecItemKind must equal backend item_kind; accents in ACCENTS.

modules.label: fe · build-run stream
modules.seam: the live build-run WS reducer; frontend counterpart of svc·build_engine event emission
modules.iface: buildRunClient consumes /api/build-runs/{id}/stream; builderUiReducer folds BuilderUIEvent sub-events (phase/plan/update_item/confirm/artifact/complete) into BuilderUIState. INVARIANT: must handle every sub-variant the backend emits or it silently drops it.

modules.label: fe · builder tab
modules.seam: the Builder tab shell + live card rendering
modules.iface: BuilderView orchestrates useBuildSpec + useBuildRun; renders PlanEditor or ProgressList+ArtifactsSection by phase. Builder ArtifactCard switches on ItemKind (agent|team|mcp|skill) + Health dot — must match backend enums.

modules.label: fe · draft cards
modules.seam: the in-chat draft-card protocol; consumes builder_ui/draft_ui events
modules.iface: draftUiReducer folds DraftUIEvent (open/field/status/link/error/cta) into DraftUIState; DraftCard switches on card_kind. INVARIANT: CardKind Literal must match backend builder_ui CardKind exactly or cards fall through to a generic renderer.
modules.tests: NONE — 1-14% line coverage; the deepest untested frontend slice

modules.label: fe · app shell
modules.seam: the tab/hash router; embeds fixed channel ids
modules.iface: App reads tab from URL hash (#/builder, #/marketplace, #/chat, ...); 6 tabs. INVARIANT: Builder + Marketplace tabs embed FIXED channel ids ('agent-builder', 'marketplace') seeded by the backend; ChannelsView hides them. Rename the seeds → tabs break.

modules.label: fe · admin views
modules.seam: the non-chat tab views (channels picker, marketplace, agents, teams, settings)
modules.iface: ChannelsView (channel/thread picker + hides fixed ids), MarketplaceView (embeds 'marketplace' channel + installed MCP/skill grid), AgentsView (topology), TeamsView, SettingsView (schema-driven form).

modules.label: build_runs_repo
modules.seam: the single owner of build_runs-table SQL; split out of api-repositories like actions_repo
modules.iface: pure SQL for the build_runs table: insert/get/list/update_status/append_event/get_events → BuildRunRow; imports _connection only. Consumed by the build_runs router worker.

modules.label: build_specs_repo
modules.seam: the single owner of build_specs-table SQL; the Stateful Builder's persistence floor
modules.iface: pure SQL for the build_specs table: insert/get/list/update/delete → BuildSpecRow; imports _connection only. Consumed by build_spec_service.

modules.label: seed · system agents
modules.seam: deep config — 5 yaml+prompt dirs define the entire default product persona set
modules.iface: core/_seeds/agents/{manager,agent-builder,web-researcher,author,installer}/ (agent.yaml + system_prompt.md.j2). Bundle-copied verbatim by init (_BUNDLED_SEED_AGENTS); small yaml interface, load-bearing behaviour (every default channel's roster).
modules.tests: data-on-disk; copy+load exercised by test_init_workspace + config-loader, CONTENT not asserted

modules.label: seed · provider catalog
modules.seam: the backend-selection catalog; agents reference these by name
modules.iface: core/_seeds/shared/providers/{claude-code,codex}.yaml — backend + init_model_string + extra_kwargs. config-loader resolves agent.llm.provider against this catalog. A hardcoded claude-code FLOOR in templates.py covers the wheel-without-bundle case.

modules.label: seed · prompt library
modules.seam: the prompt-partial library prompts-composer renders; change a role → every agent shifts
modules.iface: core/_seeds/shared/prompts/: base.md.j2 + roles/ (team_lead, worker, orchestrator, builder, installer, mission_agent, ...) + styles/ (speech, memory, shared_workspace, iron_man_speech). The behavioural contracts injected into every system prompt.

modules.label: seed · system skills
modules.seam: the built-in skills every agent can use
modules.iface: core/_seeds/shared/skills/system/{auto-ui,scaffold-any,search-catalog}/SKILL.md. Discovered by the skills loader + injected into agent skill rosters at init.

modules.label: seed · services
modules.seam: the bundled service spec the supervisor discovers
modules.iface: core/_seeds/services/kanban/service.yaml — a ServiceConfig spec the supervisor mounts. Defines the one service that ships with every workspace.

modules.label: seed · builder-team
modules.seam: the one seeded team; mounted under Manager in default, listed directly in agent-builder
modules.iface: core/_seeds/teams/builder-team/team.yaml — leader=agent-builder, workers=[web-researcher, author, installer]. Bundle-copied by init (_BUNDLED_SEED_TEAMS). The deliberate team-vs-direct-roster split is in templates.py channel yaml.

modules.label: cli · repl loop
modules.seam: the interactive mission/chat turn loop; consumes the event stream + renderer
modules.iface: repl.py runs the read→stream→render loop (mission mode shows complete_mission verdict); chat.py the channel-chat variant. Ctrl-C cancels the in-flight turn rather than killing the process.

modules.label: cli · input controller
modules.seam: the terminal-input seam; prompt_toolkit composer + slash dispatch
modules.iface: InputController.read()→str: multi-line composer, Enter-sends keybinding inversion, /-slash completion (DEFAULT_COMMANDS), persisted history, empty-resubmit loop; raises EOFError on Ctrl-D, KeyboardInterrupt on Ctrl-C (the caller's contract).
modules.tests: no dedicated test; the read()/keybinding/slash contract is unit-testable

modules.label: chat · seq allocator
modules.seam: the load-bearing seq-monotonicity + replay seam, behind a tiny interface
modules.iface: _SeqAllocator.for_thread(thread_id) returns ONE process-wide instance per thread (concurrent WS sessions MUST share it); .next() lock-guarded monotonic counter; .assign_and_persist keeps persisted seq == envelope seq (the reconnect-replay invariant). _replay_missed_events streams rows with seq>last_seq.
modules.tests: test_chat_seq_allocator.py (9 tests: for_thread shared-instance, monotonic next() under a threadpool, persisted seq == envelope seq) + test_event_replay.py. DONE.

modules.label: providers · _common
modules.seam: the single run-input assembly seam both CLI adapters call; one build() returns a typed bundle
modules.iface: ProviderBuildContext.build(cfg, resolved_prompt, mission_mode) -> frozen {workspace, model, mcp_servers, system_prompt}; composed once. model_id kept as a public leaf for the event stream. The collect_mcp_servers pass-through was inlined + deleted.
modules.tests: test_deep_providers.py (context fields: workspace/model/mcp/system_prompt + model_id leaf); both adapters stub the one seam (claude patches ProviderBuildContext.build; codex rebinds session.ProviderBuildContext)

modules.label: svc · build_run
modules.seam: owns the build-run lifecycle END-TO-END — slot registry, single-build lock, event sink, worker, AND persistence. Deeper than the runs sibling (which keeps its worker in routers.runs._helpers) on purpose — recorded in docs/adr/0001-build-run-service.md
modules.iface: BuildRunService.{list_runs,get_run,get_events,active,is_active,start,cancel,delete}; get_build_run_service() is the singleton holding the in-flight slot (_active dict + _lock). Raises typed BuildRunNotFoundError(404)/BuildConflictError(409). NOT re-exported from core.api.services package — imported via submodule to avoid the services↔build_engine import cycle.
